what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment tied to the design-source channel, pointing to an image asset via a URI and recording when it was added.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter with no body content.
It has an added timestamp of 2026-06-22T11:30:59.454626+00:00.
The filename uses the same UUID as the id field (att-79d53650.md).
standouts: References a capital-raise image asset (p-30) in the design-source channel.
Serves as a standalone markdown record for an attachment rather than embedding the image directly.
conventions: Follows a YAML-frontmatter schema with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Uses an att-<uuid>.md naming convention matching the id.
Uses an image:// URI scheme to locate the attachment asset.
concerns: No information about the uploader, image format, dimensions, or alt text is present.
The target URI cannot be resolved or validated from within this file alone.
notes: A lightweight, timestamped attachment pointer that links a design-source channel entry to an external image asset.
questions: What design-source entry or page does p-30 illustrate?
Who added this attachment and from what source?
What is the actual image format, size, and content of p-30?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a — the file does not explicitly state a purpose, but its content behaves as expected attachment metadata.
apparent_themes: Separation of metadata from binary assets
intention_signals: To catalog an image asset related to a capital-raise design artifact so it can be retrieved alongside its originating channel context.
novelty: Stores attachment metadata as a standalone markdown file and uses an image:// URI to reference the actual asset.
contribution: Provides an attachment identity, channel association, asset pointer, and creation timestamp that a fused system can use for retrieval and provenance.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter markdown attachment record (att-79d53650) in the design-source channel pointing to image://design-source/capital-raise/p-30 and recording an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog an image asset for a capital-raise design-source entry so it can be located and referenced in context.
